decision: Standalone repo published, local contract checks pass, and the first real Kaggle training run is queued next.

First Kaggle push failed in cell 1 because the attached code dataset mounted as Mini Project.zip instead of an extracted repo tree. Patch the same version to extract the archive into /kaggle/working/HNDSR-Rebuild before training.
Kernel version 8 is running after the same-version notebook patch that extracts the attached Mini Project.zip archive before training.

Second Kaggle push still failed in cell 1 because the code dataset was not mounted under the fixed expected slug. Broaden the same-version notebook bootstrap to enumerate /kaggle/input and detect the attached archive or repo under any Kaggle dataset slug.
Kernel version 9 is running after the same-version notebook patch that enumerates /kaggle/input and detects the attached code dataset under any Kaggle slug.

Third Kaggle push showed the code dataset under /kaggle/input/datasets/harshv777/... rather than a top-level Kaggle slug. Recurse through the Kaggle input tree to discover nested private dataset mounts and attached archives.
Kernel version 10 is running after the same-version notebook patch that recurses through nested /kaggle/input paths to discover private dataset mounts and attached code archives.

Kernel version 11 still invoked stale repo code from the attached dataset and failed in the readiness validator with a read-only write under /kaggle/input/.../artifacts. Re-upload the repo dataset after same-version runtime patches, then retry the same notebook version.
Kernel version 12 is running after re-uploading the attached repo dataset so the copied Kaggle workspace matches the latest same-version runtime patches.

Kernel version 12 reached the control evaluation but failed because the Kaggle image dataset is mounted under duplicated HR_0.5m and LR_2m wrapper directories instead of the flat repo-local data/kaggle_4x layout. Patch the runtime loader only and retry the same version.
Kernel version 13 is running after the runtime loader patch that resolves Kaggle's duplicated HR_0.5m and LR_2m wrapper directories without changing the vR.1 config contract.

Kernel version 13 resolved the dataset mount correctly but failed on Kaggle's Tesla P100 because the bundled PyTorch build only supports CUDA capability 7.0+. Add a conservative CPU fallback instead of changing the research contract.

Kernel version 14 produced artifacts without authenticated secret-backed W&B tracking. Under the updated policy, offline Kaggle runs are invalid evidence and must be rerun only after `WANDB_API_KEY` is enforced in-notebook.
Kernel version 15 enforced the W&B policy correctly and declined immediately because Kaggle secrets do not currently provide `WANDB_API_KEY`. Add the secret in Kaggle, then rerun the same version.

Kernel version 16 was retried after the user reported adding the secret, but the notebook still could not read `WANDB_API_KEY` from the Kaggle runtime. The secret name, notebook attachment, or permission scope is still wrong on Kaggle.

CLI-triggered Kaggle execution is now treated as upload-only for secret-backed notebooks. Official Kaggle metadata has no secrets field, so authenticated W&B runs must launch from the editor after `WANDB_API_KEY` is attached.

A manual editor-backed run reached authenticated W&B tracking but crashed in `scripts/train_baseline.py` because `training.max_train_batches` was `null` and the loop still compared `batch_idx >= None`. Patch the same version and rerun through the secret-aware editor workflow.

Kernel version 17 republishes the fixed `max_train_batches: null` handling plus the new `run-editor` and `ensure-secret` workflow. Use the editor-backed launch path for the next canonical W&B-authenticated retry.
